title: What two interruptions did the writer have?

meanings.usage: 指打扰或中断某人正在做的事情
指用于制作糕点的面团，通常含有黄油
形容让人感到烦恼或生气的事物
表示失望或担忧的情绪
通过说理使某人做某事或相信某事
门上用来转动开门的圆形把手
指需要签收的挂号邮件
描述粘性物质如糨糊、糖浆等的特性
把两种或多种东西混合在一起
形容没有声音或很安静的环境
电话响或打电话的动作

phrases.usage: 制作肉馅饼，指做一种含肉的烘焙食品

examples.en: I decided to make some meat pies for dinner.
examples.zh: 我决定做些肉馅饼作为晚餐。

phrases.usage: 拿起电话听筒，指接电话的动作

examples.en: She picked up the receiver and said hello.
examples.zh: 她拿起听筒说了声你好。

examples.en: Please ring back later when I'm free.
At last I hung up the receiver.

phrases.usage: 一...就...，强调两个动作紧接发生

examples.en: I had no sooner got back to the kitchen than the doorbell rang.
examples.zh: 我刚回到厨房，门铃就响了。

phrases.usage: 很快；不久，表示时间短暂

examples.en: In a short time I was busy mixing butter and flour.
examples.zh: 很快我就忙着搅拌黄油和面粉了。

examples.en: The doorbell rang loud enough to wake the dead.
He wanted me to sign for a registered letter.
examples.zh: 他要我签收一封挂号信。

examples.en: My hands were soon covered with sticky pastry.
examples.zh: 我的手上很快沾满了黏糊糊的面团。

phrases.usage: 就在那时，强调恰好在某个时间点

examples.en: At exactly that moment, the telephone rang.

grammar.title: 过去完成时与no sooner...than连用
grammar.definition: no sooner...than表示'一...就...'，no sooner后面用过去完成时，than后面用一般过去时，表示前一动作刚刚结束，后一动作就发生了。
grammar.structure: 主语 + had no sooner + 过去分词 + than + 主语 + 过去式动词
grammar.usage: 用于描述两个紧接发生的动作，强调时间上的紧密联系。相当于hardly...when...的结构。

examples.en: I had no sooner got back to the kitchen than the doorbell rang.
examples.zh: 我刚回到厨房，门铃就响了。

examples.en: She had no sooner entered the room than the phone rang.
examples.zh: 她一进房间，电话就响了。

examples.en: He had no sooner finished his homework than his friends called.
examples.zh: 他刚完成作业，他的朋友就打来电话了。

grammar.title: 否定最高级结构
grammar.definition: Nothing could have been more + 形容词原级 表示'没有什么比...更...'，是一种加强语气的否定最高级表达。
grammar.structure: Nothing could have been more + 形容词
grammar.usage: 用于表达某事物是某种状态中最极端的，强调程度很深。比直接用最高级更有强调效果。

examples.en: Nothing could have been more annoying.
Nothing could have been more beautiful.
examples.zh: 没有什么比这更美丽的了。

examples.en: Nothing could have been more exciting.
examples.zh: 没有什么比这更令人兴奋的了。

grammar.title: 过去进行时描述背景
grammar.definition: was/were + 动词-ing 可以用来描述故事发生的背景，为主要事件做铺垫。
grammar.usage: 在叙述过去事件时，先描述正在进行的动作作为背景，然后引入打断的事件。

examples.en: I was busy mixing butter and flour.
examples.zh: 我正忙着搅拌黄油和面粉。

examples.en: He was reading a book when the doorbell rang.
They were having dinner when the power went out.
examples.zh: 他们正在吃晚饭时停电了。

sentencePatterns.pattern: I had no sooner + 过去分词 + than + 主语 + 过去式动词

original.en: I had no sooner got back to the kitchen than the doorbell rang.
original.zh: 我刚回到厨房，门铃就响了。

imitations.en: I had no sooner sat down than the phone rang.
She had no sooner opened the door than someone knocked.
We had no sooner started the game than it began to rain.
imitations.zh: 我们刚开始比赛，天就下雨了。

sentencePatterns.pattern: Nothing could have been more + 形容词

original.en: Nothing could have been more annoying.

imitations.en: Nothing could have been more disappointing.
imitations.zh: 没有比这更令人失望的了。

imitations.en: Nothing could have been more surprising.
imitations.zh: 没有比这更令人惊讶的了。

imitations.en: Nothing could have been more wonderful.

sentencePatterns.pattern: 主语 + was/were busy + 动词-ing

original.en: I was busy mixing butter and flour.
original.zh: 我正忙着搅拌黄油和面粉。

imitations.en: She was busy writing her report.
They were busy preparing for the party.
He was busy cleaning the house.

sentencePatterns.pattern: It took me + 时间 + to + 动词原形

original.en: It took me ten minutes to persuade her to ring back later.
original.zh: 我花了十分钟才说服她稍后再打来。

imitations.en: It took me two hours to finish the work.
imitations.zh: 我花了两个小时完成这项工作。

imitations.en: It took them three days to reach the destination.
imitations.zh: 他们花了三天才到达目的地。

imitations.en: It took her five minutes to understand the problem.
imitations.zh: 她花了五分钟才理解这个问题。
